titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 31

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=112 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:00.170097+00 | translation moa=113 status=live modified=2016-06-06 15:37:23.25589+00

p.cl:
Anno ab Incarnationem domini . Millesimo . Centesimo Tricesimo quarto ad pascha . facta est hec conuencio
Inter Willelmum Cumin Clericus ex una parte . ⁊ priorem ⁊ conuentum sancti Andree ex altera .

videlicet quod dictus
Willelmus dimisit eis ad firmam ecclesiam de dul . cum capellis terris . decimis . obuentionibus ⁊ omnibus
aliis rebus ad dictam ecclesiam pertinentibus in tota uita sua reddento eidem singulis annis
sexaginta ⁊ octo marcas . Scilicet . viginti ⁊ duas marcas ad festum sancti martini . ⁊ viginti
⁊ duas marcas . ad purificationem beate marie . ⁊ viginti ⁊ vnam marcas . ad pascha . ⁊ respon
dendo de episcopalibus ⁊ sinodalibus . Et sciendum quod dicti Prior ⁊ conuentus tres marcas .
residuas sibi quolibet anno retinebunt pro tribus marcis nomine pensionis eisdem
debitis a dicto Willelmo . de dicta ecclesia de dul . Si uero contigerit quod dicti Prior ⁊ conuentus a solucione
predicte pecunie ad terminos statutos destiterint  per singulas ebdomadas a sinulis terminis
computatas nomine pene dimidiam . marcam . quousque predicta pecunia fuerit soluta propter solucio
nis dilationem persoluent .

vt autem hec conuencio robor optineat firmitatis parti huius cirographi
penes priorem ⁊ conuentum residenti apponitum est sigillum dicti . W . Parti uero penes . W. residenti
apponitum est sigillum Capituli sancti andree .

Hiis testibus . Capitulo sancti andree . Domino . M . archidiacono
Aberdon’ . Gerardo decano de fif . W . de nidin . Clerico domini regis. Et multis aliis

In the 1234th year since the Incarnation of the Lord, at Easter, this agreement was made between William Comyn the Clerk on the one side, and the prior and religious community of St Andrews on the other,

namely that the said William has handed over to them to ferm the church of Dul, with chapels, lands, teinds, obventions, and all other things pertaining to the said church by rendering to him each year for the whole of his life sixty-seven merks, that is, twenty-two merks at the feast of Saint Martin, and twenty-two merks at the Purification of Blessed Mary, and twenty-one merks at Easter, and by answering for episcopal and synodal dues. And it is to be known that the said Prior and community shall retain for themselves the remainder each year in return for three merks as a pension owed to the said William from the said church of Dul. If, however, it should happen that the said Prior and community should cease the payment of the said money at the stated terms, by every week counted from each term, they shall pay out as a penalty half a merk until the aforesaid money has been paid according to the delay of the payment.

And that this agreement might have enduring strength and durability, to the part of this cyrograph remaining in the possession of the prior and community has been affixed the seal of the said William. And to the part remaining in the possession of William has been affixed the seal of the chapter of St Andrews.
With these witnesses: the Chapter of St Andrews; the lord M(alcolm), archdeacon of Aberdeen; Gerard, dean of Fife; W(illiam) of Nydie, clerk of the lord king; and many others.
